concerns.text: Task T034 implements a normality threshold of `p ≥ 0.05` to trigger a t-test. This contradicts the Plan's 'Methodological Rigor' section which explicitly mandates a stricter `p > 0.10` threshold to avoid Type I errors on small samples. The task silently relaxes the plan's statistical safety constraint, risking invalid test selection on skewed data.

Task T026 specifies calculating coverage 'on the specific changed lines only'. While this aligns with the Plan's 'Critical Methodological Note', it contradicts the literal text of FR-004 and SC-001 in the Spec which state 'calculate code coverage for generated tests and baseline manual tests' without the 'changed lines' restriction. The task introduces a scope narrowing not explicitly authorized in the FR/SC text, creating a semantic drift where the deliverable (changed-line coverage) does not match the FR's literal requirement (general coverage).

Task T011 describes implementing a 'hard stop' for the 6-hour runtime limit. FR-007 requires a hard stop on 'cumulative execution time exceeds 6 hours OR the sample count reaches the configured limit'. The task description omits the sample count limit logic, effectively weakening the FR-007 constraint by ignoring the sample count trigger.

Task T028 marks tests as '[deferred]' if they fail to compile. FR-003 and US-2 AC-3 require the system to 'capture the compilation error' and record '[deferred] coverage'. The task description lacks the explicit requirement to capture/log the specific compilation error, which is a necessary part of the FR-003/US-2 deliverable for traceability and debugging.
